metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a pass/fail threshold to AC2 (e.g., 'agreement >= 80% across permutations passes; below fails and is recorded as an R3 blocker') so the order-shuffle experiment has a falsifiable outcome symmetrical with AC1.
evidence: AC2 says 'is recorded' with no minimum agreement percentage
AC1 by contrast has a concrete threshold: 'kappa >= 0.6 passes, below fails'
ABSENCE: no analogous 'X% agreement passes, below fails and is recorded as an R3 blocker' clause in AC2
findings.finding: AC2 (order-shuffle stability) has no pass/fail threshold — it requires only that results are 'recorded', with no agreement-rate floor defined. An executor cannot determine whether the order-shuffle experiment passed or failed, making the criterion non-falsifiable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'Order-shuffle stability for the R3 container criterion is recorded (verdict agreement across >=3 section-order permutations on >=20 container plans).'
scenarios: Executor runs order-shuffle, observes 55% agreement, and cannot determine whether to proceed or raise a blocker — both 'record and continue' and 'record as blocker' are consistent with the AC as written.
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC2 to: 'Order-shuffle stability for the R3 container criterion is recorded (verdict agreement across >=3 section-order permutations on >=20 container plans); agreement >= 80% passes, below fails and is recorded as an R3 blocker.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'capability-assertion cohort' operationally: specify which criterion tags (e.g., E4, G6, T3) or finding-text properties identify a finding as a capability-assertion finding, and add a harness step that filters the adjudication corpus to this sub-population before drawing the >=50 sample.
evidence: build_adjudication_corpus.py stratifies by `criteria[0]` (primary criterion) only — no capability-assertion cohort field exists.
Search for 'capability-assertion cohort' returns zero matches across the entire repo.
Search for 'capability cohort' returns zero matches.

The adjudication_corpus.jsonl schema fields are: finding_id, ticket_id, criterion, criteria, source, decision, drop_reason, finding, suggested_fix, location, severity, norm_id, impact, validity, priority, tp_fp, rater_a, rater_b, rationale — no cohort tag.
findings.finding: The plan asserts '~50 findings drawn from the E1 adjudication corpus (targeting the capability-assertion cohort)' as if this cohort is already identified and filterable. Reading build_adjudication_corpus.py confirms the corpus schema stratifies exclusively by `criterion` field (E4, G6, T3, etc.) — there is no `capability_assertion_cohort` label, no sub-population tag, and no filtering mechanism for 'capability-assertion' findings. The plan neither defines what makes a finding qualify for this cohort nor specifies how to extract it from the 300+ finding corpus.
findings.location: What section: '3-vote self-consistency of asserted_capability_confirmed over ~50 findings drawn from the E1 adjudication corpus (targeting the capability-assertion cohort)'; AC1
scenarios: An implementer cannot extract 50 capability-cohort findings from the corpus without a definition of what qualifies — the experiment cannot be executed as written.
If the cohort is defined as 'E4 criterion findings', the plan should say so explicitly; if it is a cross-criterion subset, the filtering logic must be specified.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Capability-assertion cohort = findings with primary criterion in {E4, G6, T3} (the cohorts defined in R5); filter adjudication_corpus.jsonl by criterion field before sampling. If the filtered population is <50, expand to include secondary criteria or supplement with fresh runs over the E4 eval suite.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'capability-assertion cohort' precisely: name the criterion/criteria (e.g. T1 with `capability_assertions_resolved`, or the G6/E4/T3 set named in the R5 research note) and add a corpus-filter step to the eval driver that selects the >=50 findings from that cohort.
evidence: adjudication_corpus.jsonl rows show criterion values: T8, G6, E6, E2, G1G2, COH, F4, T3, E4 — no 'capability-assertion' label exists.
build_adjudication_corpus.py stratifies by criteria[0] across all criteria; no cohort pre-filter for capability assertions.
docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md R5 section names G6/E4/T3 as the cohort; T1 routing JSON shows capability_assertions_resolved under T1 — plan is silent on which applies.
ABSENCE: no harness or sampling script in docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/harnesses/ targets a capability-assertion slice.
findings.finding: The 'capability-assertion cohort' required by AC1 (>=50 findings) is undefined: no criterion label, harness filter, or corpus slice exists for it. The adjudication_corpus.jsonl is stratified across all criteria (T8, G6, E2, COH, F4, etc.); 'capability-assertion' maps to T1's capability_assertions_resolved checklist item or the G6/E4/T3 cohort named in the R5 research note, but the plan never specifies which. Without this definition the eval driver cannot identify the required 50 findings.
findings.location: What section (AC1): '>=50 capability-cohort findings drawn from the E1 adjudication corpus (targeting the capability-assertion cohort)'
scenarios: Executor samples from the adjudication corpus and cannot identify which 50 findings constitute the 'capability-assertion cohort', leading to either guessing (invalidating the kappa result) or blocking on ambiguity.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section and AC1, replace 'capability-assertion cohort' with the explicit set, e.g. 'findings whose primary criterion is T1 (capability_assertions_resolved checklist key present)' or 'findings from the G6/E4/T3 cohort as defined in R5', and add a one-line filter in the harness: `rows = [r for r in corpus if r['criterion'] in {'T1', 'G6', 'E4', 'T3'}]`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify the source of the >=20 container plans for AC2's order-shuffle test (e.g. 'sample epics with >=2 child tickets from the live outcome_corpus.jsonl store') and add the selection step to the Scope or What section.
evidence: docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/ contains runs for 'container_epic' (ticket 3a03-b3f2-b34c-4e4f) as the only container-type ticket across runs.jsonl, runs2.jsonl, runs3_opus.jsonl, runs4_group.jsonl.
ABSENCE: no harness for sampling or enumerating >=20 container-type plans from the live store.
The plan's Scope section says only 'docs/experiments/ (eval driver + runs/ output + writeup)' with no mention of a new corpus-sampling step.
findings.finding: AC2 requires order-shuffle stability over '>=20 container plans', but the existing experiment corpus contains only one container_epic ticket (id 3a03-b3f2-b34c-4e4f). The plan does not name a source for the additional 19+ container plans, nor does it say whether they should be sampled from the live ticket store, constructed synthetically, or borrowed from another corpus.
findings.location: AC2: 'verdict agreement across >=3 section-order permutations on >=20 container plans'
scenarios: Executor has one container plan and cannot meet the >=20 minimum, forcing an unplanned corpus-building step or a silent under-powered run that the writeup cannot defend.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a cohort-identification rule to the What/AC1 section specifying how findings are selected from the adjudication corpus (e.g. 'criterion field in {G6, E4, T3}' or a text-heuristic), and state the fallback if that filter yields < 50 rows (e.g. expand to all G6 findings, or report sample-too-small as a blocker).
evidence: The adjudication corpus README states findings are 'stratified by primary criterion' with no asserted_capability cohort label
The corpus was descoped from TP/FP labeling (README: 'finding-level TP/FP LABELS are NOT shipped'), so no capability-assertion filter exists
R5 (research doc §R5) defines asserted_capability_confirmed as a new sub-answer never previously applied to corpus findings
ABSENCE: no selection rule (e.g. 'criterion IN [G6, E4, T3]', or 'finding text mentions existing component capability') is stated in the plan
findings.finding: The plan names a 'capability-assertion cohort' as the sample frame for AC1's 3-vote consistency test, but provides no mechanism for identifying which of the 400 unlabeled adjudication corpus findings belong to that cohort. The corpus is stratified by criterion (G6, E4, COH, T5c, etc.) and carries no 'asserted_capability_confirmed' label (that sub-answer is brand-new in R5 and was never applied to historical findings). An executor cannot determine which rows to select without an explicit filter rule, and the overall cohort may have fewer than 50 qualifying findings — the plan provides no fallback for an insufficient sample.
findings.location: What section (AC1): '>=50 findings drawn from the E1 adjudication corpus (targeting the capability-assertion cohort)'
scenarios: Executor opens adjudication_corpus.jsonl and finds no 'asserted_capability' field to filter on — has no principled way to select the cohort
Filtering by criterion=['G6','E4','T3'] yields fewer than 50 rows, leaving AC1's minimum sample unmet with no described fallback
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, add: 'Capability-assertion cohort = rows in adjudication_corpus.jsonl whose `criterion` field is in {G6, E4, T3} (the cohort R5 routes through the agentic verifier). If that filter yields <50 rows, expand to include all G6 findings; if still <50, record sample-insufficient as a blocker alongside any partial kappa.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the source and preparation of the >=20 container plan texts for AC2's order-shuffle test (e.g. 'fetch plan text from the ticket store for epic-type tickets in outcome_corpus.jsonl using the existing git-object walk', or 'author a small fixture of 20 synthetic container plans committed under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/'), and add this to the Scope section.
evidence: The runs/README.md describes two committed corpora (outcome_corpus.jsonl, adjudication_corpus.jsonl) — neither stores plan text
ABSENCE: no mention of a plan-text retrieval step, a separate container-plan corpus, or how the 20 plans are obtained
The Scope section says 'Consumes E1's adjudication corpus' — but that corpus has no plan text
findings.finding: The order-shuffle stability test (AC2) requires running the judge over >=20 container plans, but the plan does not specify the source of these plan texts. The adjudication corpus (the only committed corpus under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/) stores finding text and metadata but not the original plan text of the tickets. The outcome corpus stores per-ticket signals but also no plan text. Fetching live plan text from the ticket store requires live API or git-object access — but the plan says 'no packaged changes land from this story' and the scope is confined to docs/experiments/, leaving the data pipeline for the plan-text collection step undescribed.
findings.location: What section (AC2): 'prompt-order-shuffle stability for the R3 container criterion … >=3 section-order permutations on >=20 container plans'
scenarios: Implementer goes to run the order-shuffle harness and discovers there is no plan-text corpus to shuffle sections of
Fetching plan text from live tickets adds an undescribed dependency on the ticket store / git-object API that the Scope section doesn't acknowledge

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (and document in the plan) that the E1 adjudication corpus contains >=50 findings tagged as capability-assertion / T1 cohort; if not, specify the augmentation strategy (e.g., draw from live judge runs or a held-out set) before committing to AC1's >=50 floor.
evidence: Plan says '~50 findings drawn from the E1 adjudication corpus (targeting the capability-assertion cohort)' — treats cohort availability as given
adjudication_corpus.jsonl row sample shows criteria: G6, COH, E1, F4, T8, E2, G4, T5c, P9, T9, G1G2, E4, T4, E5 — no T1/asserted_capability_confirmed findings visible
AC1 mandates '>=50 capability-cohort findings' as a hard floor with no fallback if the corpus is undersized
findings.finding: The plan asserts the E1 adjudication corpus contains a sufficient 'capability-assertion cohort' (>=50 findings tagged asserted_capability_confirmed / T1-type) without verifying it. Corpus inspection shows only criteria G6, COH, E1, F4, T8, E2, G4, T5c, P9, T9, G1G2, E4, T4, E5 in visible rows — no T1 capability-assertion findings. If the cohort is too small, AC1 cannot be satisfied and the experiment design collapses at execution time.
findings.location: What section — '~50 findings drawn from the E1 adjudication corpus (targeting the capability-assertion cohort)'; AC1
scenarios: Executor runs the eval driver against the corpus and finds <50 T1-cohort findings, making AC1 unsatisfiable without augmenting the corpus — a scope expansion not anticipated by the plan
If the cohort is supplemented ad-hoc, the writeup's prompts-as-run record (AC3) may not reflect the actual sample provenance
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pre-condition note to the What section: 'Pre-condition: confirm the E1 adjudication corpus contains >=50 T1-criterion findings (tagged asserted_capability_confirmed); if the count is N<50, augment with [source] to reach the floor before running the eval.' Reference the count in AC1 so the executor can verify it upfront.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a pass/fail threshold to AC2's order-shuffle stability criterion (e.g., 'agreement >= X% constitutes a pass; below X% is recorded as an R3 blocker') and define what 'verdict agreement' means (which output field is compared across permutations).
evidence: AC2 text: 'Order-shuffle stability for the R3 container criterion is recorded (verdict agreement across >=3 section-order permutations on >=20 container plans)' — no threshold, no pass/fail rule
AC1 by contrast specifies 'kappa >= 0.6 passes, below fails and is recorded as an R5 blocker' — a complete decision rule
The plan says AC2 is 'the input to R3's gate row' but no agreement floor governs whether R3 is gated or not
No definition of 'verdict' for the container criterion in this plan — block/advisory/pass or binary present/absent finding?
findings.finding: AC2 specifies that order-shuffle stability results are 'recorded (verdict agreement across >=3 section-order permutations on >=20 container plans)' but defines no pass/fail threshold and no definition of 'verdict' for the R3 container criterion. An executor cannot determine what agreement rate constitutes a pass, a blocker, or a recorded-but-acceptable result — the decision protocol for this sub-step is entirely absent.
findings.location: AC2 (order-shuffle stability); What section ('prompt-order-shuffle stability for the R3 container criterion')
scenarios: 70% agreement across permutations is recorded; it is unclear whether this triggers a blocker for R3 or is acceptable — R3 ships with an unstable criterion
Executor must arbitrarily choose a threshold, making the experiment's gate decision non-reproducible

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the 'capability-assertion cohort' will be isolated from the existing adjudication corpus before R5 lands — either name an interim labeling criterion (e.g., findings from T3/G6/E4 criteria as a proxy) or add an explicit dependency that R5 must ship before E6 can run.
evidence: Plan text: '~50 findings drawn from the E1 adjudication corpus (targeting the capability-assertion cohort)'
Existing corpus file: docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/adjudication_corpus.jsonl — criteria present include T8, E2, T3, T5d, not 'asserted_capability_confirmed'
Plan text (Scope): 'Consumes E1's adjudication corpus' — implies the existing corpus is sufficient as-is
Evidence class: Contradicted — the corpus the plan depends on does not yet contain the capability-assertion cohort label that defines the sample
findings.finding: The plan draws its >=50-finding sample from 'the E1 adjudication corpus (targeting the capability-assertion cohort)' — but the existing corpus (`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/adjudication_corpus.jsonl`, ~374 rows) carries criteria labels like T8, E2, T3, T5d, etc. from the current rubric. The `asserted_capability_confirmed` sub-answer (the capability-assertion cohort) is R5's new field that has not yet landed; no such cohort label exists in the corpus today. The plan provides no method for isolating this cohort if R5 hasn't shipped before E6 runs.
findings.location: What section ('targeting the capability-assertion cohort') and AC1 ('capability-cohort findings')
scenarios: E6 runs before R5 ships; the executor cannot identify which findings belong to the capability-assertion cohort and falls back to a manual or arbitrary selection, compromising the statistical validity of the kappa result.
The cohort is defined post-hoc by re-labeling existing findings, introducing selection bias that invalidates the reliability claim.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define how the 'capability-assertion cohort' is extracted from the E1 adjudication corpus (e.g., a filter predicate on criterion ∈ {G6, E4, T3} + a human or LLM label pass identifying genuine asserted-capability findings), and confirm ≥50 such findings exist in the corpus before the AC floor is set.
evidence: Plan What: '3-vote self-consistency of asserted_capability_confirmed over ~50 findings drawn from the E1 adjudication corpus (targeting the capability-assertion cohort)'
docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/adjudication_corpus.jsonl: stratified by `criterion` field (G6, E4, COH, T8, etc.); no `asserted_capability_confirmed` field or capability-assertion tag present

ABSENCE: the plan specifies no extraction or labeling step to identify the capability-assertion cohort within the existing corpus — there is no criterion tag for this sub-answer class in the 400-entry corpus
findings.finding: The plan targets '~50 findings drawn from the E1 adjudication corpus (targeting the capability-assertion cohort)', but the existing adjudication corpus (`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/adjudication_corpus.jsonl`, 400 entries) is stratified by criterion (G6, E4, T3, etc.) — NOT by whether a finding involves an asserted-capability claim. `asserted_capability_confirmed` is a new R5 sub-answer that does not yet exist as a tag or filter key in the corpus. The plan provides no extraction method (a filter predicate, a manual label step, or a re-run) for identifying the capability-assertion cohort from the existing corpus. Without this, the '>=50' floor in AC1 may be unachievable or silently substituted by a broader, less targeted sample.
findings.location: What section — 'targeting the capability-assertion cohort'; AC1
scenarios: The implementer proxies 'capability-assertion cohort' as 'all G6/E4/T3 findings' (a superset), achieving the 50-finding floor but diluting the signal with non-capability findings — the kappa measures general judge stability, not the specific sub-answer's stability.
The implementer finds fewer than 50 genuine capability-assertion findings in the corpus and silently lowers the floor or broadens the sample, invalidating the AC1 gate.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific Fleiss kappa implementation the eval driver will use (e.g. `statsmodels.stats.inter_rater.fleiss_kappa` with an explicit dependency declaration, or a hand-rolled formula committed under `docs/experiments/`) and verify it is available in the execution environment before committing to the 3-vote design.
evidence: Plan text: 'requiring Fleiss kappa >= 0.6'
Plan text AC1: 'kappa >= 0.6 passes, below fails and is recorded as an R5 blocker'
Repo: src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:343 ships cohens_kappa (2-rater only)
Repo search: zero hits for 'fleiss_kappa' across all source files
Symbol probe: statsmodels.stats.inter_rater.fleiss_kappa → UNRESOLVED
Symbol probe: sklearn.metrics.fleiss_kappa → UNRESOLVED
Evidence class: Unverified — the Fleiss kappa computation surface is asserted but has no confirmed implementation path in repo or installed environment
findings.finding: The plan mandates 'Fleiss kappa computed' (3-vote self-consistency, AC1) but the repo ships only a 2-rater Cohen's kappa implementation (`src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:343 cohens_kappa`) and no Fleiss kappa function exists anywhere in the codebase. No third-party library providing Fleiss kappa is named, and symbol probes for `statsmodels.stats.inter_rater.fleiss_kappa` and `sklearn.metrics.fleiss_kappa` are both UNRESOLVED in the installed environment. The eval driver cannot satisfy AC1's gate row without a concrete implementation path.
findings.location: What section ('Fleiss kappa >= 0.6') and AC1 ('Fleiss kappa computed')
scenarios: The eval driver author discovers mid-implementation that no Fleiss kappa is available, either coding it from scratch (risk of math error invalidating the gate row) or pulling in statsmodels as an undeclared dependency (dependency hygiene violation).
A 2-rater Cohen's kappa is substituted silently, making AC1's '3-vote' claim technically false while the gate row passes — producing misleading reliability signal for R5 and R3.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add `statsmodels` to the experiment's dependency manifest and reference `statsmodels.stats.inter_rater.fleiss_kappa` explicitly in the What section, or (b) note that Cohen's kappa will be computed pairwise across the 3 vote pairs and averaged (a valid proxy), and update AC1 to reflect the actual method used.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how Fleiss kappa is to be computed (implement inline formula, use `statsmodels.stats.inter_rater.fleiss_kappa`, or another named approach) so the eval driver has an unambiguous implementation path.
evidence: Plan AC1: 'Fleiss kappa computed' — specifies the multi-rater statistic by name
src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:343: `def cohens_kappa(rater_a: list, rater_b: list)` — 2-rater only, takes exactly two list arguments
No `fleiss` implementation found anywhere in src/
The plan provides no instruction on how to obtain or implement Fleiss kappa
findings.finding: The plan mandates 'Fleiss kappa computed' for 3-rater agreement, but the only kappa implementation in the codebase is `cohens_kappa` in `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:343`, which is a 2-rater formula. Fleiss kappa requires a different multi-rater formula. The plan never specifies whether to implement a new Fleiss function, use a third-party library (e.g., `statsmodels`), or adapt the existing function — this decision rule is absent and not co-located with the eval schema.
findings.location: AC1; What section ('Fleiss kappa >= 0.6'); src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:343
scenarios: Executor reuses `cohens_kappa` with vote-pair averaging across 3 pairs — produces a non-standard approximation, not true Fleiss kappa, making the reported statistic scientifically invalid
Executor imports a third-party library not in the dependency set, breaking the bounded-by-small-sample cost model
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Compute Fleiss kappa inline using the standard formula (sum of observed agreement minus expected, normalized); do not reuse `cohens_kappa` (2-rater). A self-contained ~15-line helper is sufficient and adds no new dependency.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the `asserted_capability_confirmed` enum vocabulary (`yes|no|na`) in the eval driver spec and specify the `na`-handling rule for Fleiss kappa (include as third category, or exclude and require n_valid >= 50 after exclusion).
evidence: Plan: '3-vote self-consistency of asserted_capability_confirmed over ~50 findings' — enum vocabulary never restated in this plan
R5 research doc defines `asserted_capability_confirmed: yes|no|na` but this is not co-located with the eval driver spec or the kappa formula
Search of src/ confirms `asserted_capability_confirmed` does not exist in any shipped schema file — the enum is undefined in production code
No rule in AC1 or the What section specifies whether `na` votes participate in Fleiss kappa as a third category or are excluded as abstentions
findings.finding: The plan evaluates self-consistency of `asserted_capability_confirmed` votes but never defines the enum's value vocabulary (`yes|no|na`) within this plan or any co-located schema artifact — it exists only as inline prose in a research doc (task-decomposition-sota-2026.md:197). Critically, the kappa decision protocol omits a rule for whether `na` votes are included in or excluded from kappa computation: if `na` is treated as a third categorical label it depresses kappa spuriously; if excluded it silently reduces n below 50.
findings.location: AC1 (self-consistency criterion); What section ('3-vote self-consistency of asserted_capability_confirmed'); docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md:197
scenarios: Executor implements kappa with `na` as a third label: agreement is artificially depressed when the judge correctly abstains on non-G6/E4/T3 findings, producing a false kappa < 0.6 that triggers a spurious R5 blocker
Executor excludes all `na` votes: n may fall below 50, violating AC1's minimum sample size without a compensating rule
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: '`asserted_capability_confirmed` takes values `yes | no | na`; `na` votes (findings outside G6/E4/T3 cohort) are EXCLUDED from kappa computation; the 50-finding minimum applies to the post-exclusion n_valid count. The eval driver must assert n_valid >= 50 before computing kappa.'

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
no verification commands or testing plan referenced
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.

scenarios: Executor implements the eval driver and must choose a kappa library ad-hoc (statsmodels, sklearn, or a hand-rolled formula), with no plan-specified reference implementation to validate against

findings.finding: No concrete proving command or invocation pattern is described for either the self-consistency or order-shuffle evaluations; the plan names 'eval driver' and 'runs/ output' as outputs without specifying how to invoke the driver or what output schema to inspect, leaving AC1 and AC2 with no reproducible evidence-producing command.
findings.location: What / Scope / Acceptance Criteria — the eval driver is referenced but never given an invocation pattern.
scenarios: A second engineer reproducing the experiment cannot determine how to invoke the driver or which output file contains the kappa value, making the writeup the only verification artifact and removing the ability to re-run or audit the result.
If the writeup is the sole evidence artifact, a reviewer cannot independently verify the kappa computation without re-reading all the raw votes by hand.

src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:343: `def cohens_kappa(rater_a: list, rater_b: list)` — existing 2-rater implementation
No Fleiss kappa implementation found anywhere in the codebase (search returned no hits for 'fleiss', 'multi-rater', 'three-rater')
ABSENCE: the plan does not acknowledge the existing cohens_kappa or explain how Fleiss kappa will be sourced/implemented in the experiment harness
findings.finding: The plan specifies 'Fleiss kappa computed' (3-rater) but the codebase already ships `cohens_kappa` (2-rater) in `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval.py:343`. The plan's eval driver will need a Fleiss kappa implementation that is NOT present in the codebase. If the harness author reaches for the existing `cohens_kappa` as a shortcut (applying it pairwise and averaging), the math is subtly wrong for 3 raters and the gate value (kappa ≥ 0.6) would be computed on an incomparable scale. The plan should confirm that the harness will implement Fleiss kappa correctly — either inline (stdlib-only, as the existing kappa logic is) or via a listed dependency — and explicitly note that `cohens_kappa` from `eval.py` is NOT reused for this purpose.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('Fleiss kappa computed'); plan Why section
scenarios: An implementer discovers `cohens_kappa` in `eval.py`, uses it pairwise-averaged across 3 rater pairs, and reports a kappa that is numerically incomparable to the Fleiss standard — the gate threshold (0.6) then has no valid interpretation.
An implementer pulls in `statsmodels.stats.inter_rater.fleiss_kappa` as an undeclared dependency, creating an unlocked transitive dep in an experiment harness.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the What section: 'Fleiss kappa is implemented inline in the eval driver (stdlib-only math, mirroring the style of `eval.py:cohens_kappa`) — the existing `cohens_kappa` is 2-rater only and must NOT be reused for 3-vote self-consistency.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the sample-size spec: replace '~50' in the What section with '>=50' (or update AC1 to '~50') so both sections agree on the minimum count.
evidence: What: '3-vote self-consistency of asserted_capability_confirmed over ~50 findings'
AC1: '3-vote self-consistency over >=50 capability-cohort findings is recorded'
~50 could yield 48, which passes the What description but fails the AC hard minimum
findings.finding: The What section says '~50 findings' (approximately) while AC1 mandates '>=50 capability-cohort findings' (a hard minimum). These are contradictory: a run of 48 findings satisfies 'approximately 50' but fails the AC gate, sending the implementer in two directions.
findings.location: What section (part a) vs Acceptance Criteria AC1
scenarios: Implementer collects 48 findings, considers the What clause satisfied, but CI/gate check against AC1's >=50 fails — ambiguous pass/fail outcome.
findings.suggested_fix: Change What (part a) from '~50 findings' to '>=50 findings' to match the AC1 hard minimum.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the order-shuffle protocol detail (>=3 section-order permutations, >=20 container plans) to the What section (part b) so every AC threshold has a matching design description.
evidence: What (b): 'prompt-order-shuffle stability for the R3 container criterion is recorded' — no permutation count or plan count specified
AC2: 'verdict agreement across >=3 section-order permutations on >=20 container plans'
The thresholds (>=3, >=20) are orphaned to the AC with no design rationale in the body
findings.finding: AC2 introduces two quantitative thresholds — '>=3 section-order permutations' and '>=20 container plans' — that have no corresponding description in the What section. The What body says only 'order-shuffle stability for the R3 container criterion is recorded', leaving the implementer to discover the measurement protocol solely from the AC rather than the design narrative.
findings.location: What section (part b) vs Acceptance Criteria AC2
scenarios: Implementer reads What and designs an experiment with 2 permutations and 15 plans; this satisfies the prose description but fails AC2 — a preventable late-stage rework.
findings.suggested_fix: Expand What (b) to: 'prompt-order-shuffle stability for the R3 container criterion, measured across >=3 section-order permutations on >=20 container plans, is recorded'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add error-handling and partial-completion detection to the eval driver: specify minimum completion threshold (e.g., all 150 votes must succeed), retry logic for transient API errors, and a hard-fail with a logged error message if the run is incomplete — so the kappa gate is never computed on partial data.
evidence: 'Live LLM calls (billable), bounded by the small sample' — no retry/backoff mentioned
No AC or scope line specifies error handling for partial-completion scenarios
The kappa gate result directly feeds R5's blocker determination, so a corrupt partial result has downstream consequence
findings.finding: The plan invokes live LLM calls (billable) but specifies no retry, backoff, or error handling for API failures. If calls fail mid-run, partial votes may be silently incorporated into the Fleiss kappa computation, producing a misleading gate result that could incorrectly unblock or block R5.
findings.location: ## What — 'Live LLM calls (billable)'; AC line 1 (kappa >= 0.6 passes)
scenarios: LLM API rate-limit or transient 5xx mid-run: some of the 150 votes (50 findings × 3) are recorded, remainder silently dropped; kappa is computed on <50 findings and may cross the 0.6 threshold incorrectly
LLM API is fully down: experiment produces no output but no clear failure signal is defined, so R5 gate row is left in an ambiguous state
findings.suggested_fix: In the eval driver (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/), wrap each LLM call in a retry loop (e.g., 3 attempts with exponential backoff). After the run, assert len(votes) == expected_count before computing kappa; if the assertion fails, write a FAILED sentinel to runs/ and exit non-zero so CI or the human operator knows the gate result is invalid.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the eval driver emits structured per-call logs (call index, model, latency, success/error) and writes a run summary (total calls attempted, succeeded, failed, estimated cost) to runs/ alongside the kappa output.
evidence: 'Live LLM calls (billable)' — cost is acknowledged but no instrumentation is mentioned
Acceptance Criteria only require the writeup to record 'prompts-as-run, votes, and kappa math' — silent about errors or call-level telemetry
ABSENCE: no log/trace/metric line anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: No observability is specified for the LLM call phase: there is no mention of logging call counts, latencies, errors, or cost, leaving operators unable to diagnose a failed or stalled run.
findings.location: ## What — 'Live LLM calls (billable)'; ## Scope — 'eval driver + runs/ output'
scenarios: A run stalls on a slow API response with no timeout: operator has no signal that the eval driver is hung versus making progress
A run silently drops 10 votes due to deserialization errors: the writeup records kappa from 40 findings with no indication something went wrong

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `asserted_capability_confirmed` already exists in a packaged verifier prompt or must be authored as part of E6 (the plan currently treats it as pre-existing, but no such field is found in any reviewer prompt or production schema).
evidence: Search for 'asserted_capability_confirmed' returns only docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md and a .rebar/cache entry — no packaged prompt, no verifier schema, no production code.
docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md:197 defines asserted_capability_confirmed as a *proposed* typed sub-answer for R5, which 'belongs in pass 2' as future work.
R5 research recommendation is listed as a future priority item, not a completed story.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'R5's new kernel sub-answer (asserted_capability_confirmed)' as if R5 has already shipped this field in the production verifier. Probing the repo finds `asserted_capability_confirmed` only in docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md (a research recommendation) and a cache entry — it does not appear in any packaged reviewer prompt, production verifier schema, or implementation file. E6's self-consistency experiment depends on judging this sub-answer over 50 findings, but if the sub-answer does not yet exist in the judge prompt, the experiment design is measuring a phantom field.
findings.location: Why section: 'R5's new kernel sub-answer (asserted_capability_confirmed)'; What section: '3-vote self-consistency of asserted_capability_confirmed'
scenarios: When E6 runs its 3-vote self-consistency evaluation, there is no existing judge prompt that emits asserted_capability_confirmed — the experiment cannot measure stability of a field that doesn't exist yet.

If E6 is meant to precede R5's implementation (to de-risk it), the plan should explicitly state that the candidate prompt with the new sub-answer must be authored as part of E6, not assumed to already exist.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a prerequisite to the plan: 'Draft the agentic verifier prompt variant that emits asserted_capability_confirmed (as described in the R5 research doc) as an E6 artifact under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/; AC1 runs against this draft prompt.' This makes E6 the author of the candidate prompt, consistent with how E6 already authors the R3 candidate rubric.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback protocol for failed LLM sub-calls: whether to retry (with a cap), exclude the finding from kappa (with a floor on n_valid), or abort the run — and add this rule to the eval driver spec.
evidence: Plan: 'Live LLM calls (billable)' — acknowledges real API calls with no error-handling specification
AC1 requires a complete 3-vote result for kappa to be valid; a partial-vote finding (e.g., 2 of 3 calls succeed) is neither a valid inclusion nor exclusion by any stated rule
No retry, backoff, or partial-run recovery protocol is described anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The plan involves 150+ live LLM calls (50+ findings × 3 votes) but specifies no fallback for a partial or failed sub-call. If a call fails mid-run, the plan does not state whether the affected finding is excluded from kappa, retried, or causes the entire run to abort — leaving the completeness guarantee of 'n >= 50' and the integrity of the 3-vote assumption undefined.
findings.location: What section ('Live LLM calls (billable), bounded by the small sample'); AC1 ('3-vote self-consistency over >=50 capability-cohort findings')
scenarios: A rate-limit error occurs at finding #40 of 52; the run has only 39 complete 3-vote tuples — below the 50-finding minimum with no stated recovery path
2-of-3 votes are recorded for several findings; executor must choose whether to include them, silently biasing the kappa

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
